steps.title: Calculate the slope from the two points
steps.content:
The slope of a line passing through $(x_1, y_1) = (7, 21)$ and $(x_2, y_2) = (9, 25)$ is:

$$m = \frac{25 - 21}{9 - 7} = \frac{4}{2} = 2$$

steps.title: Use the slope and a point to find the equation
steps.content:
Use the slope $m = 2$ and point $(7, 21)$ in the form $y = mx + b$:

$$21 = 2(7) + b$$
$$21 = 14 + b$$
$$b = 7$$

So $h(x) = 2x + 7$. Verify with the other point $(9, 25)$: $h(9) = 2(9) + 7 = 18 + 7 = 25$ ✓. That matches choice B — B is correct.
